reviews.text: Food was good and satisfying at an acceptable price point. Service was super friendly, kind, and deserving of a generous tip. The restaurant was clean; seating was very tight (not their fault that they are popular). Each pasta was cooked to perfection, not mushy. Sauces were flavorful, and meats were fairly tender. Portions were the perfect ratios of carbs, protein, veggies. Love the roasted garlic cloves on the bread! Everyone who was with me said they would come back again.

So so yummy and worth all the hype! This place made me miss home in the best way ever. Their Godfather dish is off the charts. The crisped pancetta and goldenly crisped chicken was everything and more! The pure roasted garlic is so smooth and delectable when spread on the bread. I will say, it's a very humble establishment. The service is quick, the workers are respectable in many ways, and it's simple. It's a place for food without all the extreme over the top stuff you see at restaurants today, while also still bringing you what feels like a piece of home at restaurant level.

Ambiance was chill, many tables inside the restaurant and they definitely do fill up fast!

On January 2nd, I visited Gypsy’s Trattoria Italiana in Berkeley with a friend, and let me tell you, this place is a hidden gem.

I ordered Special #2: a huge plate of pasta in a rich, creamy pesto-style sauce with veggies, topped with tender, well-seasoned steak strips and served with hot, steamy garlic bread slathered in freshly made smashed garlic butter. It came out piping hot and smelled incredible. My friend had a different pasta dish and could only finish half—the portions are no joke. I definitely overate, in the best way.

The vibe is casual, the service is quick, and the staff are friendly and warm. For the price, the food honestly rivals what you’d get at some high-end Italian restaurants, which makes it an easy yes for me.

I highly recommend not only visiting Gypsy’s, but specifically ordering Special #2 if you see it. I’d love for them to cement this as a permanent menu item. It deserves a permanent spot! I’ll absolutely be back and plan to bring family from all over the world here when they visit Berkeley.

Honestly was not prepared for the environment that was Gypsy’s. Came on a Sunday around 9pm. The guy taking orders was pretty rude, I asked if I could have some mozzarella on added on my pasta. Went back  and fourth with the gentleman because he told me he cannot put mozzarella because theres cheddar mixed in the cheese. Which makes no sense since there is chicken parm on the menu. W.e no mozz on my pasta, cool.

Then sitting down is another task, there was people holding seats for people with a group like 8 when there is only 3 people in there group currently. While there is other people with outside food taking up multiple tables taking up space for paying patrons. One guy with a ramen another group of people eating burritos and donuts. No one managing the seating was pretty crazy to me. Cool ill sit at the table next to the door that is being held by someone smoking a cigarette in 30 degree weather.

Honestly the food was great. I was surprised how well my pasta was. All in all I would only get take out next time, I wouldn’t subject my self to this dine in mayhem again.

TLDR: You can make their meatball sandwich at home easily! Just dump a can of spaghetti-o's on some cheap bread, let it soak until soggy.

Side note - Last year I got food poisoning from their Gypsy Chicken. It sent me to the hospital.

I used to order Gypsy's Trattoria Italiana several times a week. I always ordered the Gypsy Chicken dish and it was always great until one time I ate it and I ended up hospitalized. Ever since then I've avoid eating there but eventually I came back and tried some other dishes. I've tried the chicken parmigiana, pizza, calzone and meatball sandwich - all have been subpar. I think this restaurant is cutting corners to save money and not using fresh ingredients. Hence the flavorless meat slices in my picture instead of flavorful meatballs as advertised. Nice portion sizes though and I hear the Frutti Di Mare is good but I'm too scared to try their seafood.

Dear Gypsy's, what happened? Quality matters. Please get it together. Thanks.

text.text:
People say this Italian restaurant serves delicious pasta, calzones, and Pollo con Pesto. They also highlight the generous portions, affordable prices, and quick service.

Some reviews mention the food can be inconsistent.

reviews.text:
MEZZO!! LET'S GO!! What a delicious delicious experience here. I came in when it looked a little slow, and I honestly had no clue what to get. I decided to do the BLTA on some kind of whole grain bread with the works, pickled jalapeños added, extra pickles ots, and the chipotle aioli on the side. The shorter man with a mustache who took my order made me the most delicious sandwich ever! It was genuinely everything I could have wanted. There's a perfect balance of bacon and all the vegetables on it. The mayo and dijon add to the smoothness of the avocado, and it all melts together in the best flavor profile ever. The chipotle aioli was also absolutely delicious for dipping the sandwich into. It would've been really amazing inside the sandwich as well, but truthfully it's not a necessity with all the other well balanced aspects of it. The sprouts were a surprising addition that added a little extra texture-wise and I would think nutrient wise, or at least it made me feel healthier. They were also really good.

The ambiance of the place was kind of okay. Not my favorite because it was so loud next door but the workers keep everything neat and theyre pretty friendly. I did get a mango green tea that tasted nothing like mango or green tea, but it was still a really tasty drink. It all came out to around 18 bucks, which I thought was pretty worth it. All in all, I DEFINITELY will be coming back.

I am not sure what happened on this day.

The person making my salad rubbed her nose all over her hands did not proceed to wash her hands, and then put her gloves on and started to serve me. I asked for a half-and-half vegetarian with no sprouts. She made me completely the wrong salad and put sprouts on it and when I repeated no thank you no sprouts. She just took the sprouts off and they were still sprouts in the bowl.

My salad was made in a bowl that other salads had previously been made in.

There was a stack of bowls, that have been sitting there who knows how long- that they were mixing every salad in the same dirty bowls. The bowl that my salad was mixed in had completely different dressing in it than the kind that I requested so it was all mix mixed together.

Later on, I saw someone mixing lemonade in a container on the floor.

The trash can and the place where you return your dirty dishes is right next to where they store clean dishes and where you can fill a water cup.

I’m very concerned about the health and safety of the food made at this restaurant.
Say nothing about cross contamination because clearly that is not important to them.

I cannot recommend vegetarians to eat here because it’s possible that your food will be mixed with meat without your consent.

People with food allergies should not eat here either.

Just look at the mess.

Excellent healthy, delicious sandwiches and salads. The portions are generous and the ingredients are great. Definitely opt in for sprouts and check out the different bread options, they’re all great. Nice outdoor sunny seating in rear patio

I've been coming here since the mid-80s, no matter how far away I live or work, ordering their delicious "Tossed Green" salad with poppy seed dressing or the "Combination" half sandwich and TG salad. Along with their delicious cinnamon rolls. I keep coming back because the food is delicious, service is professional, efficient and friendly... and the prices for the value is excellent!! Gotta love the big bag of bread ends, too! 😊

Huge serving size for the salads. Delicious!  Only downside is the wait standing in line.  Parking can also be a challenge.  I also had the Sausage Lentil Soup and found it had more heat than I expected.  It was flavorful, just not what I wanted.

text.text: People say this sandwich shop offers delicious breakfast burritos, sandwiches, salads, and soups, with generous portions and fresh ingredients. They highlight the great value, the sunny patio, and the friendly staff. They also like the homemade bread and the variety of options, including vegan and vegetarian choices.

reviews.text: How amazing is it to have a place like this right next to the university! Honestly, you should come here with two stomachs, because you’ll want everything at once. The portions are huge — like the chef personally decided, “You’re a student, you need to survive.” I’ve already been here twice, and I’ll definitely be back again, because the food is so good it makes you want to applaud with your spoon.

Get soup. It's big and tasty for a fair price.

I got the tomato egg noodle and it was wayyy too sweet. The other noodkes had good chew.
I would come back, but the wait is quite long (10-15 minutes) for a Chinese restaurant.
Order on iPads and quick servers.

*Fyi before leaving a review I do try places 2 times prior to making a review* Love the noodle soup here. Everytime I am in Berkely I always stop here hungry or not. Its a small hole in the wall restaurant with very small tables that are cramped together. Its a nice place but the lack of room takes away from it. Wait times vary depending on what time you get there. Also, customers are very close by, standing in line staring at their phones or at you wating their turn to finally sit down. You order and pay on a tablet once seated.  After trying almost everything on the menu, my go to is just the Beef noodle soup, spicy broth and thick noodles. It does not lack flavor and is never overly salty. The beef is always tender and noodles have the right amount of chew and taste fresh. On the side, the house wontons in chili sauce. A little salty and not really spicy in my opinion but flavorful with a good chew from the dough. Can be a little loud but once your food arrives I have noticed people stop talking to their companions and just eat. Good sign. This place is has always consistently good.

Tbh I liked it less than I expected to, but mostly just because I've never been a big fan of noodles, esp ones that are this chili oil forward. Everything was done well: beef are super tender, noodles are perfectly cooked and chewy,  broth is super flavorful - just still not my type of thing. The lamb skewers are pretty dry and salty but I do love how cumin forward they are

The beef brisket soup in the 8h broth was a 10/10, no notes. Right amount of spice and loads of flavour. It’s pretty big so we shared one. We also got the lamb skewers to go which were amazing as well. We reached 5 mins before opening on a Saturday and still had to wait about 15-20 mins.

text.text: People say this Chinese noodle restaurant serves delicious noodle soups with generous portions of tender meat and chewy noodles. They highlight the rich and complex flavors of the broth, and the reasonable prices. They also like the fast service and efficient table turnaround time.

reviews.text:
What a treat. Total hole in the wall joint serving some of the best grilled dogs you’ll ever find. No seating, it’s grab and go all the way, but that’s part of the charm. The owner has that perfect mix of crusty and friendly that makes the experience memorable.

I had the brat and the garlic frank, both fantastic, smoky, and served on a perfectly grilled sesame bun. Prices are super reasonable, especially for the quality. They take cards, but cash moves faster. Drinks are basic but fit the vibe.

You’ll probably pick up a quick dose of Berkeley street philosophy while waiting in line, which just adds to the fun. Open late, fast service, and full of character.

Bottom line a must-stop gem for anyone near campus. Grab two dogs, some chili, and enjoy the nostalgia.

Absolutely incredible, hot dogs don’t get better than this! We were on a road trip and were a tad intimidated by the long line outside. These were so worth the wait, the crispy yet chewy hot dog bun, and the flavors of the bratwurst, garlic, and the all beef frank were amazing! Best hot dog I’ve ever had!

What can I say about Top Dog that everyone else hasn't already commented on? The vibes are just as expected, and true to what should draw you in. It's an old fashioned place and it works. You go in and order a hot dog, there's a few side orders like potato salad or chili. I love chili, but I wanted to try out the potato salad. Honestly, despite it not being store bought (according to who I asked) it was definitely leaning towards that taste because it was a little sweet. However, it had a texture that made it feel classic, and I appreciated that.

I asked about their popular hot dogs and was pointed in the direction of the Hot Link. Genuinely, I was surprised over how small it was (ha ha). As someone on the shorter side, I want to struggle a bit while l eat a hot dog. HOWEVER, it was perfect for what it was, which is: a hot dog with toppings in accordance with what YOU like and what's YOUR desired amount or whatever YOU want to omit. In here, you're the "Top Dog" (unless if a worker makes you feel bad I guess, according to some other reviewers, who should just cry about it instead).

I like a place that runs a good deal. For 6-7 bucks I got a Hot Link with free toppings of sauerkraut, relish, onions, ketchup, and mustard (didn't spot the mayo or anything else I would've added it, like a person of culture), along with a potato salad side that's put in an 80z--but definitely still has space to move around--container. I devoured the hot dog on the way home. A close to 8 minute experience. The bread wasn't too soft, the perfect slight crunch, mixed with the straight of the grill hot dog was heavenly. The toppings added to the flavor of the sausage and toned down the spiciness. The sausage wasn't that big compared to the roll, so the toppings were mostly on top (I would recommend opening your own roll and placing some toppings deeper inside, or get ready to take some strategic bites).

It probably took about 6-8 minutes with ordering and waiting for it to be ready. In that time, I chatted with the man behind the counter. He seemed really nice and considerate. Very patient with my pondering and questions. Very kind to other customers who came in as well.

I will say, it's deserving of a total 4.5 stars. It's close to perfect. I would recommend spending 16 or more minutes of your lives to come and buy a hot dog (at least once) from Top Dog.

I've been here 7 years ago it's as good as I remembered. I had the polish one and the German one this time. Love the polish one a lot more. The bread was amazing not like any hotdog bun I ever had. The store is small but packed with interesting details. There were three different types of mustard and tasty toppings. This place is a must go.

Top Dog, with spots in Berkeley, Piedmont, and Oakland, is hands down one of the best hot dog joints around. It’s super local, affordable, and fast. They offer unique options like lime chicken or mango jalapeño, and even the fountain drinks are special, made with cane sugar. I’ve been to both the Berkeley and Piedmont locations, and the vibe is always great, with lines out the door even at 1 AM. I can’t get enough!

text.text: People say this hot dog restaurant serves a variety of delicious hot dogs, including bratwurst, garlic, and all-beef franks, with a crispy yet chewy bun. They highlight the reasonable prices and the fun, characteristically Berkeley vibe. They also like the friendly and professional service.
